rulings.Case Title: TOM PISANI VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 29, 2008, line 1. DEFENDANT LAMONS GASKET COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant shifted the burden of producing evidence on the issue of successor liability and plaintiff failed to present evidence sufficient to raise a triable issue of fact on this issue. Defendant's objections to declarations of Lujan and Ay are overruled. The declarations merely provide a basis for exposure to asbestos from Flexitallic gaskets. They are not relevant to the successor liability issue but would be relevant if there was a factual basis for successor liability. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD C KIRCHNER et al VS. DESIGN DEVELOPMENT GROUP PACIFIC VI, LLC A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Its Answer To Pltfs' Complaint For Damages; Memorandum Of P/A; Declaration Of Emily Chen; And (Proposed) Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 29, 2008, line 4. DEFENDANT CITY BUILDING INC'S Motion For Leave To Amend Its Answer To Plaintiffs' Complaint For Damages is granted. The policy of the State is to allow such amendments. Morgan v. Superior Court (1959) 172 Cal. App. 2d 527, 530. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: FRANCIS DAVID RYAN VS. GUNTHER DERTZ
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 29, 2008, line 15. DEFENDANT GUNTHER DERTZ'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. An easement by necessity requires a showing that plaintiff's property is landlocked and that the adjoining properties were under common ownership at the time of the conveyance giving rise to plaintiff's necessity for access. Reese v. Borghi (1963) 216 Cal. App. 2d 324, 331-332. Nor does there appear to be a basis for an implied easement. Plaintiff's other allegations are barred by the prior Small Claims Court judgment. =(302/PJM/VC)
